steps.title: Identify the subject of the blank
steps.content: The blank follows "which" in a relative clause: "Kansas City International Airport, which ______ located in Kansas City, Missouri." The relative pronoun "which" refers to "Kansas City International Airport" — a singular noun.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement — per-choice diagnosis
steps.content:
The verb must be singular to agree with the singular antecedent of "which" (the airport).
  • Choice A — "is": singular simple present. Correctly agrees with the singular noun "Airport." Correct.
  • Choice B — "are": plural simple present. Does not agree with the singular antecedent. Eliminated.
  • Choice C — "were": plural simple past. Wrong in both number (plural) and, since the airport is still located there, wrong in tense. Eliminated.
  • Choice D — "have been": plural present perfect. "Have" is plural and breaks agreement with the singular antecedent. Eliminated.
